risinfo.chaptertitle: Appendix II. Test Your Knowledge! Answer Key

listitem.para: d. G2211 is reported in addition to an office E/M code appended with modifier 25 only when reporting certain preventive services (eg, immunization administration, 90460-90461) on the same date. Do not report G2211 in addition to an office E/M code appended with modifier 25 when reporting other diagnostic or therapeutic services (eg, medication administration) on the same date.

a. True. Under Medicare's incident-to policy, code 99211 may be reported for services provided by clinical staff to established patients under the direct supervision of a physician or QHP who is either in the office suite or immediately available via real-time audio-video technology at the time of service.
b. The total time included in the code descriptor must bemet or exceeded and includes only the physician's or QHP's total time devoted the patient's care on the date of the visit.
a. The straightforward MDM supports a new patient service reported with 99202. It is not necessary to also meet or exceed the total time included in the code descriptor.

c. The office E/M code selected by the physician or QHP, based on MDM or total time, and the total face-to-face clinical staff time are necessary to determine if the time requirement for 99415 has been met. The starting point for 99415 is 30 minutes beyond the typical clinical staff time that is included in the value assigned to the office or other outpatient E/M code reported.
